Plot histogram of sample coverage using ggplot2

Source: R/plot_coverage_histogram.R
plot_coverage_histogram.Rd

Creates a histogram of total reads per sample with dynamic bin sizing. Optionally highlights samples above/below a threshold.

plot_coverage_histogram(
  table,
  threshold = NULL,
  main = "Sample Coverage Distribution",
  xlab = "Total Reads per Sample",
  col_above = "steelblue",
  col_below = "tomato",
  plot_dir = NULL,
  prefix = "coverage_histogram",
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

Arguments

table

A feature table (data.frame or matrix) with features as rows and samples as columns. First column should be feature IDs, remaining columns are sample counts.

threshold

Optional coverage threshold. Bars below this value are colored red (to remove), bars at or above are colored green (to keep).

main

Title for the plot. Default is "Sample Coverage Distribution".

xlab

Label for x-axis. Default is "Total Reads per Sample".

col_above

Color for bars at or above threshold (default: "steelblue").

col_below

Color for bars below threshold (default: "tomato").

plot_dir

Optional directory path to save the plot. If NULL, plot is displayed only.

prefix

Prefix for saved plot filename. Default is "coverage_histogram".

width

Plot width in inches for ggsave. Default 8.

height

Plot height in inches for ggsave. Default 6.

dpi

DPI for saved raster output. Default 300.

Value

Returns a ggplot object invisibly. If plot_dir is provided, also saves the plot.

Examples

data(example_feature_table)
plot_coverage_histogram(example_feature_table)

Contents
